title: verbascum × subcymosum
intro: Verbascum × subcymosum, also known as mossy mullein or cymosum mullein, is a hybrid plant species in the figwort family, characterized by its tall spikes of white or yellow flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Verbascum × subcymosum include reducing inflammation, calming coughs, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy for various conditions. Verbascum × subcymosum can be prepared into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, allowing for different methods of herbal use depending on the desired health benefits.
